title: pimpinella isaurica subsp. isaurica
intro: Pimpinella isaurica subsp. isaurica, also known as Isaurian Carrot or Turkish carrot, is a species of flowering plant in the parsley family, Apiaceae, native to Turkey. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, containing compounds such as furanocoumarins and phenolic acids, which are known for their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Pimpinella isaurica subsp. isaurica include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria and fungi. The plant can be used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety in humans.
